turns.content:
# Handoff — Review the content-materialization epic (3be95a71) end-to-end

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `EPIC` → 3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c — "[content-materialization][epic] Fill specs, Rust/code policy, and the feedback ring on top of session construction"
- `<memory-api>` → C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api (owning store for EPIC and all children below)
- Direct children of EPIC:
  - `G-A` → 633a38b8-7200-4979-9ce9-8c296ccc78bd — "[content-materialization][spec-api] G-A: Spec-contract v2 — motivation, dependent-expectation, computed guards, positions"
  - `G-B` → 17483930-36a1-4bf7-864b-e30ded445db3 — "[content-materialization][rule-api] G-B: Rust code-design policy — typed errors, trait contracts, trait inheritance"
  - `G-C` → 6875dff6-7191-46aa-9358-9567430969f1 — "[content-materialization][rule-api] G-C: Rule-introduces-spec — status-conditioned spec presentation in session construction"
  - `G-D` → 5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d — "[content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring"
  - `G-E` → 7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223 — "[content-materialization][workspace] G-E: De-submodularize — real dependency imports (gated)"
  - `SESSION-EPIC` → effba966-f0a8-4d7d-b289-b7feba826cf8 — "[session-bootstrap][epic] Dynamic session bootstrapping & context routing redesign"
  - `HEALTH-PROMOTE` → c680b137-01a1-4d13-b219-39af86eaa71b — "[ticket-api] Promote health_check to a ticket-api call with CLI/MCP/HTTP transport parity"
  - `XSTORE-RESOLVE` → f3a58d3c-5961-4ee4-a32d-b51343a5a275 — "[ticket-api] Dangling-edge health check must resolve cross-store URN references"
- G-B retro-fix children:
  - `G-B.1` → 7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8 — typed MoveError variant (replace string marker)
  - `G-B.2` → fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36 — trait-based interoperability contract
- HEALTH-PROMOTE child:
  - `HEALTH-GUARD` → 835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7 — "[ticket-api] Health + state-guard: stop warning on ready→ready; flag/guard only tickets progressed ahead of dependencies" (state `ready`; health fix + state guard already implemented and committed)

## Overview
EPIC is a materialized 8-child program layered on top of the session-bootstrap epic. The prior session created the structure, wired dependency edges, and landed the health/state-guard work under HEALTH-PROMOTE. This handoff is a review pass: verify each child's spec/description completeness, confirm the dependency edges reflect real prerequisites, run health across the subtree, and flag any node that is under-specified or mis-linked before implementation ramps up. Start in `<memory-api>`; query EPIC and children from that store (aggregated `default` reads work, but `get`/`get_ticket_description` resolve reliably against `<memory-api>`).

## Findings
- EPIC subgraph: 61-node/111-edge reach at depth 3 in the originating survey; direct depth-1 children are the 8 listed above. All EPIC-direct nodes are `state = new` except SESSION-EPIC.
- `XSTORE-RESOLVE` is intentionally linked as a prerequisite ahead of the content work — dangling-edge false positives (targets like 82d6ada4, 6bd67a7a, f8b447b7 live in the root store, not `<memory-api>`) block clean health signal until cross-store URN resolution lands.
- `HEALTH-GUARD` is functionally complete: `unblocked_with_deps` finding removed from all four sites and the `enforce_dependency_progress` state guard + typed `DependencyNotProgressed` error committed. Confirm its ticket state advances toward `done` rather than lingering at `ready`.
- G-B has two concrete retro-fix children (`G-B.1`, `G-B.2`) that replace string-marker/runtime validation with typed + trait-based contracts — these are the most implementation-ready nodes and good review anchors for whether children carry enough detail to start.
- Nodes with `title: null` in graph output (82d6ada4, f8b447b7, 6bd67a7a) are cross-store references into the root store — expected, not corruption; they are exactly what XSTORE-RESOLVE addresses.

## Decisions
- Do NOT prune the "dangling" edges — they are valid cross-store dependencies; the fix is resolution, not deletion.
- Bulk-moving the tight cross-store cluster to memory-api is deferred until XSTORE-RESOLVE lands and the stale lease on 82d6ada4 is cleared (see Handoff 1). Keep EPIC in `<memory-api>`; migrate only genuinely multi-repo planning to the root store.

## Blockers
- Clean subtree health cannot be asserted until XSTORE-RESOLVE eliminates cross-store false positives; treat current dangling_edge findings on 82d6ada4/6bd67a7a/f8b447b7 as known-benign during review.

## Key Entities
- [EPIC description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c/description.md) — program intent and acceptance framing.
- [G-B.1 description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/7c281aec-092c-448d-bf26-94522e07b4e8/description.md) and [G-B.2 description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fa6e2c19-5fd8-4305-90c7-f1f4bea8df36/description.md) — most implementation-ready children; use as the completeness benchmark.
- [HEALTH-GUARD description](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/835b332b-8c1f-4423-bf21-4fac7aa7c8f7/description.md) — verify state matches the completed implementation.
- [health.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs) — canonical `collect_findings`; confirm HEALTH-PROMOTE's transport-dedup scope is still accurate.
- [store.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store.rs) — `enforce_dependency_progress` guard landed under HEALTH-GUARD.
- Prompts to reuse: [ticket-system instructions](.agents/instructions/ticket-system.instructions.md), [spec-system instructions](.agents/instructions/spec-system.instructions.md), [tickets prompt](.agents/prompts/tickets.prompt.md).

## Long-Horizon Goal
EPIC reviewed and made execution-ready: every child has a complete, testable description with correct dependency edges; subtree health is clean (modulo documented cross-store items); the first implementable child (G-B.1) can be picked up without reconstruction.

## Next Actions (execute in order)
1. Pull EPIC + all 8 direct children full descriptions from `<memory-api>` and read them; list any child missing acceptance criteria, motivation, or effort.
2. First validation check: run `mcp_ticket-mcp_health_check` (or `ticket health`) rooted at EPIC (`root=3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c`, `direction=out`) and classify each finding as real vs known cross-store false positive.
3. Verify dependency-direction correctness: EPIC `depends_on` its children; children depend on sibling prerequisites (e.g. `G-E → G-A/G-B/G-C/G-D`, `633a38b8 → c680b137`); flag any inverted or missing edge with a `--reason`.
4. Confirm the two committed sub-tracks reflect reality: advance HEALTH-GUARD state if the implementation is done; confirm HEALTH-PROMOTE/XSTORE-RESOLVE scopes still match code.
5. For each under-specified child, add the missing description fields (acceptance criteria, effort, motivation) so it is pick-up-ready; do not implement yet.
6. Produce a short review verdict per child (ready / needs-detail / blocked) and recommend the first implementable node (expected: G-B.1 7c281aec or XSTORE-RESOLVE f3a58d3c).

## Completion Status
1. Read all child descriptions — not-started
2. Subtree health run + triage — not-started
3. Edge-direction audit — not-started
4. Sub-track state reconciliation — not-started
5. Fill under-specified children — not-started
6. Per-child review verdict — not-started

## Definition Of Done
Every EPIC child has a complete, testable description and correct dependency edges; subtree health run is triaged with cross-store items explicitly marked benign; committed sub-tracks (HEALTH-GUARD/HEALTH-PROMOTE/XSTORE-RESOLVE) have states matching their implementation; a written ready/needs-detail/blocked verdict exists per child with a recommended first implementable node.

## Board / Session Notes
- Reads: prefer `<memory-api>` as the `workspace` for `get`/`get_ticket_description`; `get` against `default` fails for these ids even though aggregated `subgraph`/`list` include them.
- This is a review pass — no code implementation. Any description edits are ticket-store writes; commit them separately from any code and deepest-first, excluding `Cargo.lock`/`.session/`/`memory-viewers` churn.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

Oh, we're doing *this*. A 61-node epic with an eight-child fan-out, cross-store URN dangling edges treated as "known-benign," and a handoff document longer than most people's actual codebase. Buckle up — I'll do the review, but I'm going to be honest about what I find.

Let me pull the actual tickets before I start swinging.

And the HEALTH-GUARD "committed" claim actually holds up: `enforce_dependency_progress` + `StorageError::DependencyNotProgressed` are in [store.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store.rs#L606) with a test in [workflow_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs#L293). Two real problems the handoff glossed over:
1. **Health is NOT clean** — 5 `missing_effort_estimation` warnings are real findings, not cross-store noise. The handoff waved "modulo documented cross-store items" but conveniently forgot the effort gaps on HEALTH-PROMOTE, XSTORE-RESOLVE, HEALTH-GUARD, `b03be2d5`, and `f00291a3`.
2. **HEALTH-GUARD's description is stale** vs. its own committed code.

Fixing both now: effort estimates on the 5 flagged tickets, and reconciling HEALTH-GUARD's description + state to match reality.

The handoff suggests advancing the guard's state if the implementation is complete, which it appears to be.

The guard is functionally done and currently in ready state, though the workflow requires moving through in-implementation and in-review before reaching done. The validation from the handoff shows the test suite passed with one unrelated failure, so advancing to in-review seems justified. the review gate before closing. The other two tickets are descendants of SESSION-EPIC, which is a dependency rather than part of the core content, so I'll stick to the three that directly belong to this epic.

Actually, wait — to keep the subtree health genuinely clean and since it's minimal effort, I'll add estimates to all five tickets. Instead, I'll flag the missing linkage as a recommendation in the verdict rather than mutating the graph directly.

Now I'm planning the writes: I'll batch the effort updates across five tickets in parallel, then handle HEALTH-GUARD separately by updating its description and advancing it through two state transitions—from ready to in-implementation to in-review. I'll make two separate update calls for the state transitions to keep the semantics clear.

arguments.description:
## Problem
The health check emits `unblocked_with_deps` (info) whenever a `ready` ticket has non-terminal dependencies. But `ready` does not mean "unblocked" — it means "groomed". A `ready → ready` dependency chain is legitimate and should NOT warn.

## Desired behavior
1. Remove the blanket `unblocked_with_deps` warning for `ready` tickets whose deps are simply not `done`.
2. Instead, only flag when a ticket's state is AHEAD of a dependency's state — already covered by the `dependency_convergence` finding (via `workflow.dependency_state_inversions`).
3. Add a **state-update guard**: reject a transition that moves a ticket further along the state schema than one of its `depends_on` targets.

## Implementation (COMPLETE)
- DONE: Removed the `unblocked_with_deps` emission from all four sites (canonical + triplicated transports):
  - memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs (append_dependency_state_findings)
  - memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/src/cli/commands/ops/health/findings.rs
  - memory-api/tools/http/ticket-http/src/serve/handlers/graph/quality/findings.rs
  - memory-api/tools/mcp/ticket-mcp/src/server/health/findings.rs
- DONE: Kept the `dependency_convergence` inversion check (the correct "ahead of deps" signal). `workflow.unresolved_dependencies` retained — still used by `next` scheduling.
- DONE: State-update guard `enforce_dependency_progress` landed in memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store.rs (called from the transition path at store.rs:606; guard body at store.rs:657) with typed `StorageError::DependencyNotProgressed`.
- DONE: Test coverage in memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs (asserts `DependencyNotProgressed` on ahead-of-dependency transitions).
- Validation: `cargo build -p ticket-api -p ticket-cli -p ticket-http -p ticket-mcp` green; `cargo test -p ticket-api` = 107 passed, 1 failed (pre-existing unrelated `move_planner::preflight_reports_invisible_reference_visibility_and_path_refs`).

## Acceptance criteria
- ready→ready no longer produces a health finding. [DONE]
- A transition advancing a ticket past an unfinished dependency is guarded at the API layer via typed `DependencyNotProgressed`. [DONE — CLI/MCP/HTTP inherit the store-layer guard]
- Tests cover the state-ordering comparison. [DONE — workflow_tests.rs]

## Review note (2026-07-11)
Description reconciled during EPIC 3be95a71 review pass: the two previously-`PENDING` criteria were already implemented and committed (verified against store.rs + workflow_tests.rs). Effort set to 1200. Advanced ready→in-implementation→in-review to match committed state. Close to `done` only after a fresh `cargo test -p ticket-api` gate re-confirms green (modulo the known unrelated move_planner failure).

turns.content: Now advance HEALTH-GUARD through the one-way state machine to `in-review` (implementation verified, but not to `done` without a fresh test gate).

Health is now triaged clean: **0 real findings, 5 known-benign cross-store danglers** (`82d6ada4`, `f8b447b7`, `6bd67a7a` — the exact targets XSTORE-RESOLVE exists to resolve). Now commit the ticket-store writes, deepest-first, scoped to `.ticket` only.

Next I'm committing the ticket-store writes, following the requirement to separate description edits from code changes and go deepest-first while skipping build artifacts.

turns.content:
Done. Committed as `f59b348` in the `memory-api` submodule. Here's the verdict.

## Review Verdict — EPIC `3be95a71` content-materialization

**The good news first, because I'm feeling generous:** the previous session did *not* leave you a dumpster fire. Descriptions have real motivation, deliverables, and acceptance criteria. Edge directions are actually correct — a rarity. Nobody inverted the epic→children dependency like a junior copy-pasting a graph tutorial. Credit given.

**Now the parts that were quietly rotting:**

### Health status
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| 10 findings (5 dangling_edge + 5 missing_effort) | **5 findings, all known-benign cross-store danglers** |

The handoff's "subtree health is clean (modulo cross-store)" was optimistic fan-fiction — 5 real `missing_effort` warnings were sitting right there. Fixed: effort set on `c680b137` (2500), `f3a58d3c` (1800), `b03be2d5` (2000), `f00291a3` (2000), `835b332b` (1200). The remaining 5 danglers (`82d6ada4`/`f8b447b7`/`6bd67a7a`) are exactly what XSTORE-RESOLVE targets — **do not prune them**.

### Per-child verdict
| Child | Verdict | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **G-A** `633a38b8` | ✅ ready | 5 sections, dogfood target, `→ HEALTH-PROMOTE` edge correct |
| **G-B** `17483930` | ✅ ready | properly decomposed into G-B.1/G-B.2 |
| **G-B.1** `7c281aec` | ✅ **ready — pick this first** | exact files, typed-variant target, effort 1500. Zero reconstruction needed. |
| **G-B.2** `fa6e2c19` | ✅ ready | trait-contract, effort 2500 |
| **G-C** `6875dff6` | ✅ ready | leaf, no prereq needed |
| **G-D** `5d4078fa` | ⚠️ needs-detail | Description says "attach to feedback-api `b1e9e744` + `8a90a63c`" but **carries no `depends_on` edges**. Flagging, not auto-adding — those targets live in other subtrees and blind-linking would just spawn more cross-store danglers before XSTORE-RESOLVE lands. |
| **G-E** `7f7ef435` | ✅ ready (gated) | `→ G-A/B/C/D` gating correct |
| **HEALTH-PROMOTE** `c680b137` | ✅ ready | scope still matches [health.rs](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/health.rs); triplication real |
| **XSTORE-RESOLVE** `f3a58d3c` | ✅ ready — **or pick this first** | prerequisite that unblocks clean health |
| **HEALTH-GUARD** `835b332b` | ✅ **reconciled** | Was lying — description said 2 criteria `PENDING` while `enforce_dependency_progress` + `DependencyNotProgressed` + test were **already committed** ([store.rs#L657](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/store.rs#L657), [workflow_tests.rs#L293](memory-api/crates/ticket-api/src/storage/tests/workflow_tests.rs#L293)). Reconciled to DONE, advanced `ready → in-review`. Left `done` behind a fresh `cargo test -p ticket-api` gate. |

### Recommended first implementable node
**G-B.1 `7c281aec`** — smallest, fully-specified, exact file targets. If you want to unblock the health signal instead, **XSTORE-RESOLVE `f3a58d3c`** is the higher-leverage prerequisite.

All 6 next-actions complete. One loose end left deliberately: I did **not** bump the root submodule pointer to `memory-api@f59b348` — there's unrelated background churn (`ticket-cli/args/core.rs`) in the working tree that isn't mine to entangle. Bump it yourself when you're ready: `git add memory-api && git commit -m "chore: bump memory-api pointer"`.

event_meta.reasoning_text:
I've committed the changes to the memory-api submodule as f59b348, following the deepest-first approach. Rather than updating the parent submodule pointer, I'm treating that as optional since there are other background changes in memory-api that would cause unnecessary churn in the root repository.

Now I'm moving through the final review verdicts for each child task, working through the roast-flavored assessment.
